name: Tony Bennett
abstract: Anthony Dominick Benedetto (August 3, 1926 – July 21, 2023), known professionally as Tony Bennett, was an American jazz and traditional pop singer. He received many accolades, including 20 Grammy Awards, a Lifetime Achievement Award, and two Primetime Emmy Awards.

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Mead
about_quotes.evidence: The mention of Tony Bennett occurs in the interviewer's turn ('...with Tori who went on with Tony Bennett and all that.') as part of a question about Ronnie Zito's family. The interviewee does not mention Tony Bennett in their response, nor does he indicate any personal relationship with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Mead interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, June 1, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Hamilton
about_quotes.evidence: The transcript mentions Tony Bennett only in the context of Billy Exner having played with him; the interviewee (Chico Hamilton) discusses his relationship with Billy Exner, not Tony Bennett.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Hamilton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Kirchner
about_quotes.evidence: They listened to mainstream adult pop music. So I mean they would have on the radio what the records of the time were, maybe Sinatra things or Tony Bennett or Barbra Streisand
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Kirchner interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 4, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Candido Camero
about_quotes.evidence: I went to work with Tony Bennett and also we toured the entire United States... I recorded with him a number of times too
about_quotes.doc_title: Candido Camero — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John Bunch
about_quotes.evidence: I held the job for six years... I played a few tunes behind him. He says, “You got the job if you want it.”
about_quotes.doc_title: John Bunch interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: George Masso
about_quotes.evidence: It was called a Special Service Band of Europe. Tony Bennett was one of the singers.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Masso interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 10, 1997
